result:
This is a feature-rich terminal-based Game of Life simulator with pattern recognition, scripting, multiplayer, GIF export, time-travel, and more — all in a single 2400-line file.

**Goal: Add a GPU-accelerated compute backend using NumPy/SciPy convolution so the simulation can handle massive grids (1000x1000+) at interactive frame rates, with automatic fallback to the current cell-by-cell engine when NumPy is unavailable.**

The current engine iterates cells individually in Python, which becomes a bottleneck on large grids. A convolution-based backend would compute the neighbor count for the entire grid in one vectorized operation, delivering order-of-magnitude speedups — the kind of change that unlocks entirely new scales of simulation without breaking any existing features.
